panels.description: Medium shot: Alice sits thoughtfully on the floor of the mysterious hall, looking up with a pensive expression. Soft, ambient lighting creates a contemplative mood. The background subtly hints at the strange, empty hall.

bubbles.text: But if I'm not the same, the next question is, Who in the world am I? Ah, _that's_ the great puzzle! And she began thinking over all the children she knew that were of the same age as herself, to see if she could have been changed for any of them.

panels.description: Medium close shot: Alice, looking intensely puzzled, holds her chin with a thoughtful gesture. Her brow is furrowed as she struggles to recall her identity and basic facts. Subtle light source from above casts gentle shadows on her face, highlighting her internal struggle.

bubbles.text: I'm sure I'm not Ada, for her hair goes in such long ringlets, and mine doesn't go in ringlets at all; and I'm sure I can't be Mabel, for I know all sorts of things, and she, oh! she knows such a very little! Besides, _she's_ she, and _I'm_ I, and—oh dear, how puzzling it all is! I'll try if I know all the things I used to know.

Let me see: four times five is twelve, and four times six is thirteen, and four times seven is—oh dear! I shall never get to twenty at that rate! However, the Multiplication Table doesn't signify: let's try Geography.

panels.description: Medium close shot: Alice with a slightly open mouth as if reciting, her expression then shifting to mild confusion and a slight frown. Her head is tilted, eyes wide in disbelief. The background softly suggests the mysterious hall, keeping focus on her bewildered face.

bubbles.text: How doth the little crocodile Improve his shining tail, And pour the waters of the Nile On every golden scale! How cheerfully he seems to grin, How neatly spread his claws, And welcome little fishes in With gently smiling jaws!
I'm sure those are not the right words,

panels.description: Medium close shot: Alice's face shows a mix of determination and growing sadness. A tear might be welling in her eye, reflecting her increasing distress. Soft, melancholic lighting casts a somber mood. The background is slightly blurred to emphasize her emotional state.

bubbles.text: I must be Mabel after all, and I shall have to go and live in that poky little house, and have next to no toys to play with, and oh! ever so many lessons to learn! No, I've made up my mind about it; if I'm Mabel, I'll stay down here!
I do wish they _would_ put their heads down! I am so _very_ tired of being all alone here!

panels.description: Close-up shot: Alice's eyes are wide with sudden fear and dawning realization. Her body is slightly distorted, beginning to shrink rapidly, creating a palpable sense of unease and panic. Dramatic, stark lighting from below casts long shadows, enhancing the fearful atmosphere.

bubbles.text: I must be growing small again.

panels.description: Medium shot: Alice, now visibly smaller, reaches futilely towards the small, shut door. The golden key is prominently visible on the large glass table. Her expression is one of deep disappointment and despair, shoulders slumped in defeat. Overcast, gloomy lighting emphasizes her predicament.

bubbles.text: And in that case I can go back by railway,

and alas! the little door was shut again, and the little golden key was lying on the glass table as before, 'and things are worse than ever,' thought the poor child, 'for I never was so small as this before, never! And I declare it's too bad, that it is!'

panels.description: Dynamic wide shot: Alice is captured in mid-air, having just slipped, splashing dramatically into a large pool of water. Her face displays a vivid mix of shock and surprise, eyes wide and mouth agape. The water ripples violently around her, indicating the force of her fall. The environment is dark and watery, enhancing the sudden, disorienting impact.

bubbles.text: Her first idea was that she had somehow fallen into the sea, 'and in that case I can go back by railway,' she said to herself.
However, she soon made out that she was in the pool of tears which she had wept when she was nine feet high.
